safe_checks: After a rising clock edge with rst=1, the FIFO must externally reflect empty state: empty=1, full=0, items=0, slots=13, push_ready=1, pop_valid=0.
Accepted push is defined only by push_valid && push_ready on the rising clock edge.
Accepted pop is defined only by pop_valid && pop_ready on the rising clock edge.
When the FIFO is empty and rst=0, and push_valid=1 with pop_ready=1, the module must assert pop_valid combinationally and drive pop_data=push_data in the same cycle.
When the FIFO is empty and bypass is not active, pop_valid must be 0.
When the FIFO stores at least one item, pop_valid must be 1.
When the FIFO stores at least one item, pop_data must equal the oldest stored item.
When the FIFO is full at 13 stored items, push_ready must be 0.
When the FIFO is not full, push_ready must be 1.
Buffered pushes append data to the tail in FIFO order.
Buffered pops remove exactly the oldest stored item on the active clock edge.
After a buffered pop, the next queued item becomes visible on pop_data on the following cycle.
items must equal stored queue depth in stable states where occupancy is unambiguous.
slots must equal 13-items in stable states where occupancy is unambiguous.
full must be 1 iff stored depth is 13 in stable states.
empty must be 1 iff stored depth is 0 in stable states.
Simultaneous accepted buffered push and accepted buffered pop must preserve occupancy while maintaining FIFO ordering.
In empty state with push_valid=1 and pop_ready=0, the item must be stored for later delivery rather than bypassed.
unsafe_checks: Checking exact values of full_next, empty_next, items_next, or slots_next during reset cycles.
Checking exact *_next values during empty-bypass cycles unless the intended one-step effect is unambiguous.
Assuming items/items_next include same-cycle bypass-only traffic.
Assuming empty/full/items/slots include same-cycle combinational adjustments at every boundary case.
Requiring exact same-cycle combinational status values during empty-to-nonempty or nonempty-to-empty edges beyond explicitly guaranteed cases.
Requiring the old head to remain on pop_data after a buffered pop edge.
Assuming any specific internal pointer movement or memory read/write ordering.
Checking reset overlap with active push/pop traffic beyond the guaranteed post-edge empty-state outputs.
Checking exact status arithmetic during simultaneous push/pop at ambiguous boundaries unless observed from clear pre-edge stable occupancy.
minimal_safe_oracle: Reset-edge check: after asserting rst through a rising edge with push_valid=0 and pop_ready=0, verify empty=1, full=0, items=0, slots=13, push_ready=1, pop_valid=0.

Empty-bypass check: from known empty state with rst=0, drive push_valid=1, pop_ready=1, vary push_data, and verify same-cycle pop_valid=1 and pop_data=push_data; after the edge, verify FIFO is still empty.
Empty-store check: from known empty state, drive push_valid=1, pop_ready=0, push_data=D; after the edge verify one-item stable state with empty=0, full=0, items=1, slots=12, pop_valid=1, pop_data=D.

Buffered-pop timing check: from known one-item buffered state, drive pop_ready=1 and verify the head item is observed on the handshake cycle before the edge; after the edge verify empty state on the following cycle.
FIFO-ordering check: store multiple distinct bytes without pops, then pop them with pop_ready=1 and verify order matches insertion order.

Simultaneous buffered push/pop check: from non-empty stable state, perform push_valid=1 and pop_ready=1 and verify pre-edge pop_data is old head, occupancy stays constant across the edge, and newly pushed item appears only after older queued items are drained.
Full-boundary check: fill to 13 stored items, verify full=1 and push_ready=0 in stable full state, attempt push while full and verify no extra item is accepted and ordering is unchanged.

reset_tests.description: Synchronous reset from idle: hold push_valid=0 and pop_ready=0, assert rst before rising edge, then check reset-visible outputs only after the rising edge.
Reset recovery: deassert rst after one reset edge and verify FIFO remains empty until a real push is accepted.
Reset priority over normal operation: with buffered contents present, assert rst=1 on a rising edge while push_valid/pop_ready are idle and verify reset clears occupancy.

vectors.precondition: FIFO contains at least 2 stored items

normal_tests.description: Empty bypass basic operation: direct zero-cycle transfer through empty FIFO.
Store one item when empty and consumer not ready.
Basic FIFO ordering with multiple buffered items.

corner_case_tests.description: Boundary 0->1 transition with stable status checks.
Boundary 1->0 transition using one buffered pop.

vectors.precondition: stable full state containing 13 known bytes

corner_case_tests.description: Simultaneous buffered push and pop at occupancy>1 should keep occupancy constant and preserve ordering.
Single-entry simultaneous push and pop should return old head on the handshake cycle and leave exactly the new item buffered afterward.
Alternating data patterns and extreme byte values through buffered path.

sequence_tests.description: End-to-end state traversal: RESET -> EMPTY -> BYPASS transfer -> EMPTY -> one buffered item -> multi-item buffered -> simultaneous push/pop -> near-full -> full -> drain to empty.
expected_trace: After reset edge, empty state visible.
Apply empty-bypass cycle and verify direct transfer with no stored occupancy afterward.
Apply empty store cycle and verify one-item buffered state.
Push additional items to reach multi-item state and verify head visibility.
Perform simultaneous buffered push/pop and verify occupancy unchanged and ordering preserved.
Fill from stable 12-item state to stable 13-item full state and verify push_ready=0.
Attempt blocked push while full and verify no acceptance.
Drain all items and verify ordered outputs and final empty state.

sequence_tests.description: Pop timing sequence to separate combinational-head bugs from registered-output bugs.
expected_trace: Load two buffered items A then B.
Before first pop edge, observe pop_data=A.
Accept first pop on rising edge.
Accept second pop on next rising edge.
Following cycle returns to empty with pop_valid=0.

sequence_tests.description: Status-focused stable-state sequence for safe next-state checks.
expected_trace: Reset to empty and verify empty/full/items/slots.
Store one item and idle one cycle; verify items=1, slots=12, empty_next=0, full_next=0, items_next=1, slots_next=12.
Request pop of that one item and verify pre-edge empty_next=1, items_next=0.
Build 12-item stable state, then request one push without pop and verify pre-edge full_next=1, items_next=13.
Idle in full state and verify full=1, push_ready=0.

random_tests.description: Run constrained-random multi-cycle testing with a reference queue model. Bias generation toward occupancy boundaries 0, 1, 12, and 13; include random push_valid, pop_ready, and push_data each cycle; periodically force known states via reset. Strictly check only guaranteed behaviors: reset-visible outputs after reset edges, empty-bypass direct transfer, buffered FIFO ordering, push blocking when full, pop_valid gating on empty/non-empty, and stable-state items/slots/full/empty relations when occupancy is unambiguous. Include targeted random bursts of simultaneous push_valid=1 and pop_ready=1 both in empty and non-empty states to expose bypass and occupancy bugs.

key_discriminators: Empty FIFO with push_valid=1 and pop_ready=1 must produce same-cycle pop_valid=1 and pop_data=push_data; this catches missing or delayed bypass logic.
Empty FIFO with push_valid=1 and pop_ready=0 must buffer the item and deliver it later; this catches lost-write or false-bypass mutants.

Buffered pop must show the old head on the handshake cycle, while the next item appears on the following cycle; this separates combinational-head behavior from one-cycle-late or too-early update bugs.
Simultaneous buffered push and pop must keep occupancy constant and preserve FIFO order; this catches +/-1 occupancy mistakes and pointer update errors.
Full state must deassert push_ready and reject further pushes; this catches full-flag and acceptance bugs.
Stable-state checks at occupancies 0, 1, 12, and 13 expose off-by-one errors in items, slots, empty, full, and safe *_next predictions.
Using distinct byte patterns such as 0x00, 0xFF, 0xAA, 0x55, and ordered tags 0x10/0x20/0x30/0x40 exposes stale-output, wrong-head, and ordering mutants.
Reset must only be checked after the active clock edge because reset is synchronous; this avoids overspecifying immediate combinational reset behavior.
Do not check pop_data when pop_valid=0; this prevents false failures from stale or don't-care data.
Do not make same-cycle empty/full/items/slots behavior during bypass the primary oracle; instead check the guaranteed transfer and post-edge occupancy.
